title: NOC 21399 – Other Professional Engineers | GTR Immigration
description: Engineers classified under NOC 21399 are in growing demand across public and private sectors in Canada, covering specialized disciplines such as biomedical, agricultural, marine, textile, and engineering-science roles. This occupation falls under TEER 1, making it highly suitable for:
duties: Design machinery and systems for farming and food processing
Develop diagnostic equipment and assistive medical devices
Conduct applied-science research and process development
Design vessels, propulsion systems, and offshore structures
Engineer fiber-production processes and manufacturing equipment
employment_requirements: Bachelor’s degree in relevant engineering discipline
Master’s or PhD for advanced roles (sometimes)
job_outlook: 👉 This indicates favourable immigration prospects, particularly for overseas engineers with niche specializations and licensure readiness.
additional_information: Specialist engineers continue to be recruited in agriculture technology, medical devices, marine systems, textiles, and applied-science research.
Typical annual earnings range between $46,200–$136,200 CAD, depending on province, licensing status, and field of expertise.
